programme.desc: In Almería, father and son Steve and Shaun Garner are on a high-stakes hunt with couple Andy and Kathy, who are racing to find a forever home after accidentally selling their house in the UK. With contrasting tastes - modern shine versus rustic charm - can the Garner duo strike the perfect balance?

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: Paul travels the French Riviera by train, taking control of a steam engine on the Pine Cone line, and stopping off to ride a historic Solex motor-bicycle and visit Frances other D-Day landing site. He rides along the coast from Nice and in the cab of millionaire businessman Paul Ricards private line, and takes in the beauty of the flamingos and bulls of Provence

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.title: Big Mood

However, when Nicolle reveals that shes a people pleaser who finds it hard to say no, Dr Lori warns the couple that Nicolle needs to vocalise her feelings. The pair invite resident couple Kiki and Sophia to spend some intimate time with them in the hot tub, and the fun continues in the bedroom. But is Nicolle as happy with the nights sexual climax as she seems? Meanwhile, thrill-seekers Katey and Dan pursue a live sex show experience

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home

He also visits a grand Georgian manor built in 1820 and frequented by King Edward VII. The Worthing property was divided into flats in the 1980s and it is in one of these that owners Charlie and Julie are wrestling with how to turn a big Regency reception room into contemporary living and dining space

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son embark a 3,000-mile journey through Europe, aiming to experience extremes from the fiery heat of the Mediterranean to the icy tundras of the Arctic Circle. They begin in Croatia, where they swing off the roof of Hajduk Splits football stadium, before diving headfirst into the crystal-clear waters of the Adriatic. The action continues when the pair go head-to-head on aqua bikes alongside a former world champion, before heading to the capital Zagreb to meet a young inventor, who stuns them with his electric creation

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son head to Hungary, where they take to the open waters of Lake Balaton for a spot of high-speed catamaran sailing with an Olympic crew, before warming up in their RV with some goulash. programme.desc: Joey tries to cover up Chandler and Monicas relationship - but only succeeds in making himself look stupid. Phoebe persuades Rachel to take a literature class with her, while Ross falls out with his co-workers, convinced one of them stole his prized sandwich

programme.sub-title: The One with the Inappropriate Sister

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Recording artist Darius Rucker goes undercover to find Americas next great musical act. Darius first gained fame as lead vocalist and rhythm guitarist of rock band Hootie and the Blowfish, selling over 16 million records and winning two Grammys. He then went on to have a successful solo career as a county music artist. Now Darius wants to find talented but undiscovered new music acts and help launch their careers, so dons a disguise to work at an open mic night, as a roadie and a busker in his efforts to locate fresh songwriting and performing talent

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur Marcus Samuelsson goes undercover to find and mentor Americas next great chef. An Ethiopian refugee adopted by a Swedish family, Marcus developed a deep love for cooking at an early age and has since become a renowned force in international cuisine, with over 30 restaurants worldwide. Marcus disguises himself to work incognito in a teaching kitchen, a food truck business, a culinary institute and a soup kitchen, as he searches for undiscovered chefs to mentor

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: The DIY duo are back for series four! You know the drill: expect funky furnishings, hardware hysteria and, of course, problematic properties. Can Jonathan and Drew reverse more housing screw ups? David and Siobhan are ready to create a bidding war in their citys red-hot real estate. But first, they need the brothers to work their magic. What tricks do Jonathan and Drew have up their sleeves to secure a sale and land the couple their perfect property in one of the most expensive neighbourhoods in town?Short:David and Siobhan keep getting outbid in their citys intense market. What tricks do Jonathan and Drew have up their sleeves to secure a sale and land the couple a perfect property?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: City slicker Corey and his wife Angela kick off the series of dinner parties in Somerset at their grand Georgian home, serving up an Asian-themed menu inspired by their passion for travelling. Day two sees stay-at-home-dad Brian and his wife Kelly prepare traditional British dishes, but Corey criticises every course on the menu. Finally, outspoken Australian Shannon and her husband Tom host the third evening, hoping to satisfy hard-to-please Corey with their European cuisine, and win the cash prize

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Farm dwellers Nikki and Clive kick off the contest in East Anglia, serving up a menu of rustic fare. Self-confessed recluses, this is their first dinner party, so it remains to be seen how they cope having company round. They are followed by young couple Lloyd and Molly, who serve up a Thai menu, and bringing up the rear are Pete and Kirsty, with an evening themed around the letter P. Can they pocket the prize with perfect points? Its doubtful, but as ever itll be fun seeing how they do

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Fashionista groom-to-be James arranges a cutting-edge, catwalk fashion show ceremony full of the latest trends for his and his fiancees big day. However, bride-to-be Jenna is dreaming of a traditional and timeless wedding that will never go out of style

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: As the wedding planning show gets an upgrade, grooms-to-be are now given £14,000 to plan every detail of their nuptials - although they must still do so without any input from their significant others. The latest run gets under way by introducing Andrew, who hopes a big-top-themed wedding will distract his bride-to-be Jenni from the troubles she faces on a daily basis. Jennis confidence has taken a knock since she developed a disability caused by pregnancy, and while Andrew became her dutiful carer, he admits romance has taken a back-seat, and hopes an extravagant wedding will help him show his fiancee the spark is still there

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Walk-Ons is a sports bar chain with plans to double in size, but whether its 4,000 employees are ready to make this audacious move is unclear. Founder Brandon Landry and co-owner, NFL champion New Orleans Saints quarterback Drew Brees hope to find out by hiding in plain sight, joining staff on the front line in their restaurants. They meet a kitchen porter, an assistant manager and a dock supervisor for their main shrimp supplier, in a bid to get to the heart of their business. In the end, Brandon and Drew reveal their true identities and help make their employees dreams a reality.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Clean Harbours is a hazardous waste disposal company, dealing with everything from hurricanes and oil spills to the 9/11 clean-up operation. Its chairman and founder Alan McKim is struggling to avoid losing highly trained employees as the business expands, so he goes undercover as a mechanic to discover the reason why

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to New Orleans to visit Zekes, a much-loved restaurant that has seen standards slip since the original owner died. The new proprietors, husband and wife duo Darryl and Ellen Cortello, focused all the efforts on the financial side of things, so it is up to Gordon to show them that they first need to love food to make any profit

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: The DIY duo are back for series four! You know the drill: expect funky furnishings, hardware hysteria and, of course, problematic properties. Can Jonathan and Drew reverse more housing screw-ups? After two decades in their home, single mum Paula and her daughter Isabel are ready to move on. But their property needs a lot of help. And Paulas struggling with stress and losing sleep, so she needs a last-minute pep talk.Drew becomes doubly determined to discover dream digs. Short: Jonathan helps Paula and her daughter Isabel with their property. But with Paula struggling with stress and losing sleep, she needs a last-minute pep talk. Drews determined to discover her dream digs

programme.title: Below Deck
programme.desc: Ben deals with the fallout from disrespecting Captain Kerry, while the crew do their best to end the season strong. Kyle struggles to keep his clothes on, to Barbies dismay

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets more tasks to Dara Ó Briain, Fern Brady, John Kearns, Munya Chawawa and Sarah Millican, the intrepid players hoping to become the 14th Taskmaster champion. Dara shows off a fabulous flair for folding, Fern complains about being forced to tidy up and Munya tries to work some DIY magic with a coat hanger. Plus, Sarah beautifully reveals what kind of foodstuff is like catnip to her. As usual, Alex Horne monitors the participants progress with due diligence

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a combination of the comedy panel show and the long-running words and numbers quiz, inviting Jon Richardson and Jack Dee to take on Lee Mack and Rose Matafeo in a festive-themed edition. Theyre joined by guests Stevie Martin and Joe Wilkinson, and Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the anarchic take on the classic numbers-and-letters game, with Countdown regulars Rachel Riley and Susie Dent. This edition sees Jon Richardson and Harriet Kemsley take on Lee Mack and Munya Chawawa, while David ODoherty joins Susie in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark is intent on winning the woman of his dreams, regardless of the fact that she already has a husband, and goes to some extraordinary lengths to do so - including a joint trip to the skin clinic for mole-mapping. On the other hand, Jeremy is determined to get his life on track and not ruin his new relationship. However, trouble seems to follow him around, and when Super Hans asks him to help out a friend, it could spell the end for the El Dude Brothers. Starring Robert Webb and David Mitchell

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: When a music track Jeremy and Super Hans wrote in the noughties becomes an unexpected hit in Germany, the pair fight over the rights to it. Although their friendship has broken down, it appears that Jez has unwittingly done something good, and he is left seeking revenge. Meanwhile, Mark has a play date with his son in a soft play centre, but things do not go to plan when his disastrous love life gets even more complicated and Ian gets lost in the giant kid farm. Starring Robert Webb and David Mitchell

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers
programme.desc: Pash helps Liam lose an immature inking, Sketch finds Adams ice-cream tatt a little hard to swallow, Alice lights up Amandas life, and Uzzi rids Stefen of a terrible tribute to brotherly love. On top of all that, Megan wants to eradicate an exs initials, and Peter shows off a tattoo done by a 10-year-old

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets more tasks to Dara Ó Briain, Fern Brady, John Kearns, Munya Chawawa and Sarah Millican, the intrepid players hoping to become the 14th Taskmaster champion. programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a combination of the comedy panel show and the long-running words and numbers quiz, inviting Jon Richardson and Jack Dee to take on Lee Mack and Rose Matafeo in a festive-themed edition. Theyre joined by guests Stevie Martin and Joe Wilkinson, and Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: YouTube star and internet sensation Bethany Mota goes undercover to meet up-and-coming social media hopefuls who are trying to break through. Disguised as an ordinary girl with a punk rock style who dreams of starting her own channel, Bethany meets a world champion beat-boxer, a pair of comical twin pranksters and an inspiring hip hop dance teacher. In the end, Bethany reveals her true identity in a surprising way and helps make their dreams come true

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: American football and baseball star Deion Sanders goes undercover to meet inspiring sports coaches and deserving fans. Disguised as a businessman who hopes to start a youth football programme, Deion meets two coaches who work with inner-city kids and a remarkable fan with a really surprising story. In the end, Deion reveals his true identity to reward their generosity and help turn their dreams into reality

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: The DIY duo are back for series four! You know the drill: expect funky furnishings, hardware hysteria and, of course, problematic properties. Can Jonathan and Drew reverse more housing screw ups?Although their kids love their back garden pool, Melanie and Dereks poorly designed property gives them a sinking feeling. Its time for the family to move on. And who better to help than the property brothers? Jonathan and Drew dive headfirst into helping, but will the process go swimmingly? Short:Although their kids love their back garden pool, Melanie and Dereks property gives them a sinking feeling. Jonathan and Drew dive headfirst into helping, but will it go swimmingly?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: East Anglia is the setting as another three couples try to prove they are the best when it comes to throwing a dinner party. Flashy Paul and Jill kick things off with an Indian buffet, inspired by their regular trips abroad. On day two, young lovebirds Tom and Bonnie try to save their bacon with a breakfast-themed evening, before gym bunnies Neil and Emma swap weights for scales in an attempt to live up to t heir guests high expectations. But who will grab the grand?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sherry and her partner Phil kick-off the contest in Oxfordshire, with a traditional menu with a modern twist served in their grand dining room. On day two, competitive couple Jade and Luke rely on their menu of happy hearty food as their winning ticket. On the final night, Zahra and Conall plan to impress their guests with plates of perfectly presented food. Old smoothie Phil gives romance-shirking Conall a few lessons in love, before a few embarrassing pet names and the £1,000 prize winners are revealed

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Steve takes turning 40 as his cue to seize the initiative in arranging his wedding to partner Nicki. He aims to recreate their early 1990s courtship with a Guns N Roses-themed ceremony that caters to their shared love of rock. However, unbeknownst to her would-be-husband, Nicki only pretended to be a fellow rocker to catch Steves eye. She is actually dreaming of a traditional affair at a stately home, and may not be as welcoming of a rock video-themed big day as her fiance believes

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Childrens entertainer Mark wants to stage a theatrical wedding on a cruise ship for his bride-to-be Kelly. However, having waited 10 years to tie the knot, she is hoping for a traditional event and may not be enthusiastic when she discovers the outrageous plans he has for their big day

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Dippin Dots is an American brand that sells cryogenically frozen beads of ice-cream. Recently, it enjoyed its busiest summer, scooping 146 billion dots and making an annual revenue of $300million. But, before that, in 2012, the company was on the brink of bankruptcy, until owner and CEO Scott Fischer turned its fortunes around. Here, he infiltrates his workforce to find out if his expanding empire has the manpower to cope. As his alter ego Brad, a man looking for a new career, he goes undercover, meeting a factory production operator, a warehouse supervisor, a sales supervisor and a server

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Stacy Anderson, the brand president of global business Anytime Fitness, poses as a potential franchise owner to make sure all companies using the name are offering the same experience. She meets a gym manager, a personal trainer and a franchise owner to see if they company is fighting fit

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the restaurants he tried to save, returning to Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro to find out whether his advice has been put to good use

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Pasadena, California, where he visits Jamaican-themed restaurant Cafe Kingston. The business in run by Dr Una Morris, a micro-managing owner who also works at a radiotherapy clinic next door, and is determined to introduce hospital-style techniques to the kitchen by sealing everything in plastic bags

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: In the fifth season, more couples put their homes in the bros hands. Can Jonathan and Drew raise the roof off of dilapidated, draughty and dingy digs? Aubrey and Jonathan are out of their depth on their 10-acre property complete with horses. Can our Jonathan help spruce up their 200-year-old farmhouse? And will Drew help them get back in the house-hunting saddle? Short:Aubrey and Jonathan are out of their depth on their 10-acre property. Can our pro bros help spruce up their 200-year-old farmhouse and get them back in the house-hunting saddle?

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay strives to turn around the fortunes of Puerto Rican restaurant El Cantito. The establishment, located in Yonkers, New York, has struggled to stay afloat post-Covid amidst the breakage of key appliances, staff problems and such unfortunate instances as uncooked chicken being served. Can Gordon turn things around?

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Sarah Millican gets her hands in some cottage pie, Fern Brady is introduced as Brandy Alexander, and Munya Chawawa demonstrates a mastery of martial arts. Elsewhere, John Kearns survives an operation, and Dara Ó Briain carefully selects a luggage trolley. Greg Davies and Alex Horne assess their efforts to win the prized golden head

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.sub-title: Are We Going to Be Alright?
programme.desc: Jeremy is turning 40 and, as he struggles with the pace of his blossoming romance, he finds that three-day drug binges are not as easy as they were when he was in his 20s. He takes extreme measures in order to keep up, but Mark is determined to make him realise his age. Meanwhile, Marks job and love life hang in the balance, so he makes an audacious attempt to win the woman of his dreams. Jez and Super Hans try to help, but they only end up adding to his problems, and the El Dude Brothers bow out on a high. programme.title: Rick Steins Fruits of the Sea

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree highlights four of her favourite chicken recipes, including classic white chicken enchiladas and an all-in-one Italian chicken sheet pan supper

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Over the past seven editions, Jamie Oliver has given some of the nations favourite dishes a twist, reinventing British classics like shepherds pie, rustling up outdoor treats and transforming old-school desserts such as pavlova and sticky-toffee pudding. For his final menu, he makes fish and chips with an India Pale Ale batter, a treat for vegetarians with his version of the Indonesian salad gado gado, and a speedy crab linguine

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gives more of the nations favourite dishes a twist, continuing with lasagne with roasted duck, the ultimate bacon sandwich and, from rural Mexico, huevos rancheros - cooked eggs with a spicy tomato salsa, black beans, avocado and a soft tortilla

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.sub-title: Cook Like A Pro: Make-Ahead Desserts
programme.desc: Ina demonstrates how to prepare very professional fruit desserts in advance, with recipes including apple pie bars, chocolate banana cream pie, and stewed rhubarb and red berries

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina shares professional tips for chocolate. Recipes include chocolate ganache cake, chocolate creme brulee, peanut rocky road bark and frozen hot chocolate

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding welcome 12 contestants to the tent, where judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith oversee them in three challenges. The competition kicks off with Biscuit Week, and the bakers are challenged to make signature bakes that say something both about them and a place in the British Isles, before tackling one of Pauls childhood favourites in the Technical. Finally, the bakers are tasked to make a 3D biscuit portrait in their first Showstopper

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its week two and the remaining 11 bakers face cake week - kicking off with a crowd-pleasing signature challenge. Next, the bakers face Prue Leiths first technical, a complicated cake with a very unusual ingredient. Finally, an ambitious chocolate-collared showstopper turns up the heat in the tent. Who will rise to the various tasks - and who will melt under the pressure? Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding present, while series regular Paul Hollywood and Prue pass judgement before another baker leaves the contest

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Riddiculous
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh hosts the quiz where contestants take on Riddlemaster Henry Lewis in an attempt to solve a series of riddles - packed with themed general knowledge questions and confusing conundrums, only one team can make it through to the high-stakes final with a chance to win an exciting cash prize. How will Faisal and Daniel, Sara and Kaitlin, and Vicki and Richard cope under pressure?

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: The fast-paced game of words hosted by Adil Ray. Fighting it out today are Natassja and her Auntie Ashley from Northern Ireland, and married couples Jay and Shruti from Hertfordshire and Ian and Mary from Devon

programme.desc: A meek scientist becomes obsessed with his work, unaware he is driving his neglected wife into the arms of a conniving friend. The doctor develops a potion that unleashes his debauched alter ego, who throws himself into the debauched underworld of Victorian London before seeking violent revenge on those who have wronged them both. Hammer Horror, starring Paul Massie, Dawn Addams and Christopher Lee

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve is mystified when a sniper comes up with an airtight alibi for the attempted assassination of a government prosecutor. Gary Lockwood guest stars

Sci-fi thriller, starring Joe Lando, Claudia Christian, Cooper Harris, Brendan Bradley, Sueann Han and Gregory Paul Smith

However, he has to contend with the consequences when the actions of an arrogant hotshot pilot lead to the death of a fellow veteran airman. Drama, starring John Wayne, John Carroll, Anna Lee and Paul Kelly

programme.desc: An American naval reserve pilot is called up for service in the Korean War, and must leave his family and civilian job as a lawyer. In command of a corps of naval pilots, he heads a bombing campaign on strategic Communist positions, but later comes to question the morality of the conflict. Adventure, starring William Holden, Grace Kelly, Fredric March and Mickey Rooney

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Kirk laughs off suggestions that a respected actor is really the tyrant responsible for a massacre he saw as a child. However, a shocking discovery changes his mind and sets him off on a quest for the truth. William Shane and Arnold Moss star

However, he risks corrupting his spirit as he is drawn further down the path of violence, while the organisers of the arena have their own sinister plans for him. Action adventure, starring Tiger Hu Chen and Keanu Reeves, who also directed. In Cantonese, Mandarin and English

He reunites with a motley group of one-time criminals, but unfortunately they botch the assignment and a hitman is sent to finish them off. Thriller, starring Andy Garcia, Christopher Walken, Treat Williams, Gabrielle Anwar and Steve Buscemi

programme.title: Cowboy (1958)
programme.desc: A Chicago hotel clerk befriends a visiting cowboy and soon talks his way into joining him on a cattle drive so he can impress the woman he loves. The tenderfoot has no idea of what awaits him as he ventures out into the Wild West, an experience that forces him to undergo a dramatic change. Western, starring Jack Lemmon, Glenn Ford, Anna Kashfi and Brian Donlevy

programme.title: Sitting Bull (1954)
programme.desc: During events leading up to the Battle of Little Big Horn, a cavalry officer strives to stop the vainglorious General Custer taking on the legendary chieftain, only to end up being court-martialled for his efforts. Western, starring Dale Robertson, Mary Murphy, J Carrol Naish and Iron Eyes Cody

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Federation outposts in a galactic neutral zone are destroyed by an invisible vessel, forcing the Enterprise crew to repel an attack by the mysterious Romulans. William Shatner and Leonard Nimoy star

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Rudy Wells, the doctor responsible for Steve Austins bionic limbs, is kidnapped by a network of international criminals

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: A hospital is besieged by desperate criminals, leading to a tense hostage situation - with Stacy and Hookers ex-wife among their number. Can the crusading policeman save the day before blood is spilled? Police drama, starring William Shatner

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Bootleggers kidnap two successful country musicians in an effort to profit from the royalties of their hits, and the Rangers must rescue them as thoughts turn to murder

programme.title: Sudden Death (1995)
programme.desc: Terrorists seize control of an ice-hockey stadium in the middle of a match attended by the US vice president. Taking the politician hostage, they threaten to blow up the building at the final whistle - but a resourceful former fireman takes it upon himself to thwart their dastardly plot. Action thriller, starring Jean-Claude Van Damme, Powers Boothe, Raymond J Barry and Whittni Wright

programme.title: Death Wish (1974)
programme.desc: A family man takes the law into his own hands when his wife is murdered and his daughter is left psychologically traumatised by thugs. Setting out on a one-man crusade to rid the streets of crime, he becomes a hero to the people - and a thorn in the side of the authorities. Vigilante thriller, starring Charles Bronson, Hope Lange and Vincent Gardenia

programme.title: The Escapist (2008)
programme.desc: A convict serving a life sentence is resigned to never being released, until he learns that a drug overdose has left his estranged daughter seriously ill. Afraid she will die before he can make his peace with her, he decides to tunnel out of prison - but is forced to make some dangerous bargains to carry out his plan. British thriller, starring Brian Cox, Damian Lewis and Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: Mercenaries (2011)
programme.desc: A former SAS commando leaves the British army to become a soldier for hire, operating out of the Balkans. He is recruited by the US authorities for a top-secret rescue mission when their ambassador to Serbia is taken hostage by a former general and wanted war criminal during a coup. Action adventure, with Robert Fucilla and Billy Zane

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A young mother suffers a panic attack while out with her three-year-old son. When paramedics arrive, shes gasping for breath. A two-year-old whos running a high temperature and has a history of respiratory problems needs to go to hospital - and fast. A deaf woman has collapsed in her bedroom and the crew needs to find out why shes fallen, though she cant understand what theyre saying. A 999 caller reports a man lying unconscious at a bus stop, but finding the patient proves difficult. Plus, paramedics are called to a man whos recovering from knee surgery

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Cameras follow Lawrence, 26, who is rushed to St Georges after being hit by a car while crossing the road on a night out in Eastbourne. Meanwhile, 53-year-old Katrina is brought to AandE after having difficulty breathing and experiencing chest pains that could indicate a heart attack

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Hartlepool, the heli-medics treat a man whos been hit by a train. Cyclist Trevor suffers serious injuries after a collision with a car. Plus, in Cumbria, 12-year-old motocross biker Arai is flown across the country for emergency surgery on a deep chest wound

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Ninety-one-year-old Cyril is airlifted to hospital after a car crash which required him to be cut from his car. Meanwhile, the medical team treat 57-year-old Caroline, who arrives at St Georges with chest pains, plus five-year-old Roman has a pencil stuck in his ear

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The Food Unwrapped team explore the snacks aisle. Kate Quilton visits Kenya to find out how many mangoes are in a snack pack, Helen Lawal learns the mystery of the fluffy centre of a snowball, John Whaite cracks open the secret of caramel popcorn, and Andi Oliver investigates what a supermarket aisle means when youre shopping online

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo head to Herefordshire to meet George, who has been collecting for more than 70 years. Max finds a crate of vintage motor oil bottles that he thinks could be worth a small fortune

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In the picturesque Devon countryside, Henry Cole and Simon OBrien meet Derek and Daphne, who have decades of collecting stored in multiple barns. Henry finds a very rare tractor, which he has never found in a shed search before, while a Raleigh Shopper bike really lights Simons fire

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie welcome their guests to the Watersedge Guest House in Barton-on-Sea, Hampshire, but an afternoon of deer-watching leaves some of them bored. Desperate to impress their fellow BandB owners, the pair host a Hawaiian barbecue, before pulling out all the stops at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owners Tim and Amelia Whitaker welcome their guests to their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast, and take them out on a boat for a spot of seal watching and diving. However, the sight of wet suits proves too much for one contestant, and tensions also flare over dinner when Mike and Danny are quizzed about their relationship

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eva Buschmann hosts the competition at the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset. She makes the mistake of assuming Danny Robinson and Mike Gibson are a couple, and offers them a double room with an exposed shower, but hopes a lesson in abstract art and a meal at her favourite harbour-side restaurant will win the other contestants over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike Gibson and Danny Robinson host their rival BandB owners at their four-star guesthouse - Homecliffe Hotel in Blackpool. The hosts take their visitors on a tram ride along the seafront and on to Madame Tussauds, but there are complaints about uncomfortable mattresses and disappointing views and the next days breakfast fails to impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and air their grievances. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie start the proceedings, but confront the guests who complained about their room having no en-suite facilities, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five cooks from in and around Harrogate, North Yorkshire, battle to win the £1,000 prize in the culinary contest. First to take on hosting duties is farm girl Jane Steele, who hopes a combination of innovative recipes and fancy wine will win over her guests - horse enthusiast Cassy Bell, photographer Simon Meyer, country gent Alasdair Moore and adventurous punk and cupcake seller Cathy McConaghy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast Cassy Bell hosts the second dinner party in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, hoping her jockey-themed evening and gastro-pub menu will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cathy McConaghy hosts the third dinner party in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, where she decides to return to her Irish roots and serve a menu of home-made soda bread, cockle soup, oysters, roast lamb, and stout-infused sticky toffee pudding as she vies for the £1,000 prize. However, not all of her guests are impressed with the choice of food being dished up

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Simon Meyer is the fourth dinner-party host in Harrogate, North Yorkshire. He takes a laid-back approach to the task, but his chicken soup starter goes down so well the guests comments leave him struggling to retain his composure

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Country gent Alasdair Moore hosts the final dinner party in Harrogate, and hopes to impress his guests with his specially selected wines and proper menu. While his monkfish is well received, an early slip-up dents his confidence, and a risky parlour game does not endear him to farm lady Jane Steele. As the evening progresses, his competitors rate their experience, before the winner of the £1,000 cash prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Black Lion in Halland, East Sussex, where host Nigel Fright gets off to a shaky start when a kettle goes missing from one of the rooms. Later, he takes the guests to a salsa class, where Sherrill Tacy and Hayley Cotton are surprised by some of his dance moves

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to the Wheatsheaf Inn in Exning, near Newmarket, Suffolk, where guests are welcomed by owner Ken Clutterbuck and employee Ginny Trow. The proprietor tries to win his rivals round with his unique sense of humour, but tensions rise when the contestants go for a spin on a go-kart track

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sherrill Tacy hosts at the Sherwood Guesthouse in New Brighton on the Wirral, and is praised for the establishments value for money. However, there is upset when her breakfast leaves a lot to be desired, and her final review is far from perfect

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Final hosts Mark and Rachael Dann welcome the contestants to the Big Bear Lodge in Shropshire. They face the tough job of trying to keep the rift between some of the guests to a minimum in their ski-themed BandB, and their afternoon activity of canoeing raises a few laughs but accusations of cheating occur when the boys begin to get competitive

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants featured throughout the week reunite to revisit the highs and lows of their various stays, and as the BandB owners confront their critics face to face, they discover what the rival parties really thought of the quality of service that they encountered. After the deliberations are over, the winning guest house is announced

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help first-time buyers from Manchester. Robyn and Alice are struggling to decide between a starter flat and classic Corrie-style redbrick home. Steph and Christopher have been outbid 10 times even though theyve bid as much as £50,000 over asking price

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go head to head to help a couple who cannot decide to do with their home in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire. Procurement manager Paul bought the house 13 years ago as a bachelor pad, but since cabin crew worker Sophie moved in they have had two children and he wants to move to a bigger, better family home. However, she grew up on the same street, her parents are just down the road and she loves having them on hand for babysitting. Can Kirstie make the necessary renovations to persuade Paul to stay? Or will Phil find a new good enough home to tempt Sophie away?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Marcus and Abi, who are building a luxurious off-grid family home deep in 20-acre woodland in Pembrokeshire on a budget of just £100,000. Granted permission through the One Planet Development planning laws, which are unique to Wales, they are armed with determination and timber from the surrounding forest. With no detailed plans, the project pushes the limits of design and endurance, and proves a raw, physical adventure that defies convention and challenges many of the ideas around what a home should be

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A five-year-old is rushed into St Georges struggling to breathe, and her mum reveals how she has spent months in Great Ormond Street Hospital after being diagnosed with a rare form of juvenile arthritis. Meanwhile, an 83-year-old man in a confused state arrives in AandE after falling over, and as doctors carry out tests and a CT scan, his wife helps them understand the reason behind his confusion. Plus, a twelve-year-old girl must be brave as she undergoes treatment for a nasty cut on her leg

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Yorkshire, Neil needs urgent medical care after falling from his motorbike. The medics scramble to the Lake District when a walker goes into a cardiac arrest on the banks of Ullswater. Meanwhile, in Berkshire, Darran suffers nasty burns in an accident lighting a bonfire

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A young mother suffers a panic attack while out with her three-year-old son. When paramedics arrive, shes gasping for breath. A two-year-old whos running a high temperature and has a history of respiratory problems needs to go to hospital - and fast. A deaf woman has collapsed in her bedroom and the crew needs to find out why shes fallen, though she cant understand what theyre saying. A 999 caller reports a man lying unconscious at a bus stop, but finding the patient proves difficult. Plus, paramedics are called to a man whos recovering from knee surgery

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: In search of the secret to the bubbles in bubble tea, Kate Quilton visits a cassava farm in Kenya to get to the root of it. Jimmy Doherty is in Somerset exploring why sour milk is sneered at, though soured cream is revered. Plus, Helen Lawal reports from Portugal on canned fish

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo head to Worcestershire where Max transforms old air tanks into a sports car, while Simon makes a trendy bar bench out of old barrels

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to the north of Wales where they meet Sue, who is clearing out her late husbands antique shop stock. Henry manages to find a pedal car, while Simon takes a toy yacht

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of the musically themed Harmony BandB in Torquay, Devon, begin the competition, trying to prove their establishment offers the best value for money. Dutch hosts Hans MacDaniel and Monica van der Meij try to impress their guests, but it remains to be seen what they will make of wheelchair badminton, saxophones at breakfast and a controversial sausage

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at Chimneys BandB in North Walsham, Norfolk, where owners Jenny and Richard Harmers decision to use a flag as a tablecloth angers Hans MacDaniel and Monica Van Der Meij. The argument seems to abate during an afternoon canoeing session - only to resurface at breakfast the following day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Beverley Conoley and Caroline Pocklington host at the Wordsworth Hotel in Cockermouth, Cumbria. They take their guests to a spa and Hans MacDaniel happily volunteers for a spray tan, while Richard Harmer is far more uncomfortable as he finds himself on a massage table. The day ends with dinner at a nearby bistro, but Jenny Harmer complains about the temperature

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Lesley and Emily Gray play host at the Park Farm Guest House in Gateshead, but while the evenings entertainment featuring a ride on a two-wheeled personal transporter goes down well among the guests, tensions simmer when the dinner conversation turns to a delicate issue

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the weeks competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ptol Slattery hosts the first dinner party in Winchester, Hampshire, but his menu of seared scallops, lamb steak with baby new potatoes followed by chocolate mousse leaves his guests - waitress Patrician Pearson, DJ Francis Leigh, care-home worker Georgia Groves and estate agent William Tilsley - underwhelmed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Francis Leigh hosts the next dinner party in Winchester, hoping to dazzle his guests with his sunburnt chicken curry, a Thai meal with a twist and a dessert simply called sticky. The entertainment for the night proves to be weird and wonderful as he dons a tutu and fur coat to serve the main course

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Waitress Patricia Pearson hosts the next party in Winchester, but the decision to write her menu in Spanish causes confusion for one guest who mistakes lamb for llama. The Peruvian-themed feast is followed by a rendition of the countrys national anthem and a traditional dance involving a candle and a donkeys jaw

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night of the competition in Winchester, Georgia Groves plans a black-tie evening complete with a red carpet, posh frocks and a spray-tan booth. It appears to be going well until Patricia Pearson reveals what she thinks of Ptol Slattery and his wine antics

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent William Tilsley throws one last dinner party as a week of competitions in Winchester, Hampshire, draws to a close. The host plans to offer his guests a vegetarian menu, with a blind food-tasting game providing the evenings entertainment. As the night draws to a close, one of the competitors rises above the rest to become the winner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at One4 BandB in Cheltenham, where debonair art-dealing host Nick Purchase hopes to prove his that his establishment offers the best value for money. However, things get off to a less-than-ideal start when a life-drawing class leaves one of his guests in a fit of nervous giggles

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to the Windy Bottom BandB in Maidstone, Kent, where owners Gloria Barnett and Dave Goulding try to impress their guests with a trip to Leeds Castle. However, a proprietor manages to offend one of her rivals, and another is not impressed at being allocated a Versace-themed room

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the Jolly Carter pub in Bolton, Greater Manchester, run by Phil and Angie Sutcliffe. Despite a few nerves about the accommodation not measuring up, the room inspections go well, but the guests verdict on the afternoons roller disco remains to be seen

programme.title: Four in a Bed

With Nurse Camilla often working nightshifts, the pair are struggling to fit in some house hunting, after some time with Phil there are two contenders but some quick decisions must be made to avoid losing out yet again

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go head to head as they meet Nikki and Bash from Birmingham. After living in their three-bed house for 15 years Bash thinks its time for them and their three children to move to a sizeable home in the countryside. While Nikki agrees the house isnt right for them she thinks with the help of Kirstie and their budget of £80-100,000 they can make it work and not have to move away from friends and family. Phil takes his budget of £650,000 and searches for a house that will make them both happy

programme.title: The Orient Express: A Golden Era of Travel
programme.desc: The train leaves Paris and heads east to jewellery-making Pforzheim, Munich, a city haunted by its dark past, and Salzburg, a city that pays tribute to its famous musical family in a most unexpected way

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Mechanic Grant, 34, is rushed in after being crushed against a brick wall by his own car, which he was working on at the time. Doctors are concerned he has a serious pelvic injury and other internal wounds and work quickly to establish how severe they are. Meanwhile, 44-year-old Doug, who has a rare growth on his head and face which is prone to profuse bleeding, arrives in AandE, and 23-year-old cage fighter Charlie is treated in minor injuries for something in his eye

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Three-year-old Louie is airlifted to St Georges after being run over by a car while playing outside, with doctors expressing concern about his neck, which seems to be stuck in one position. Elsewhere, 14-year-old Josh is treated after injuring his ankle when riding a BMX with his father on the front. Meanwhile, 41-year-old Sam arrives in resus with a cut to her head and possible spinal and internal injuries after losing control of her car and crashing into a tree

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Cambridgeshire, the heli-medics race to treat a young cyclist with a time-critical brain injury. On the Norfolk Broads, holidaymaker Tom breaks his thigh bone jumping off a boat, while Cedric, an 87-year-old pilot, is put into an induced coma after he crashes his microlight in Cumbria

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A five-year-old is rushed into St Georges struggling to breathe, and her mum reveals how she has spent months in Great Ormond Street Hospital after being diagnosed with a rare form of juvenile arthritis. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits a farm in Cornwall to get to the bottom of the flower element in a in cauliflower. programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Yorkshire, Neil needs urgent medical care after falling from his motorbike. The medics scramble to the Lake District when a walker goes into a cardiac arrest on the banks of Ullswater. Meanwhile, in Berkshire, Darran suffers nasty burns in an accident lighting a bonfire

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Hartlepool, the heli-medics treat a man whos been hit by a train. Cyclist Trevor suffers serious injuries after a collision with a car. Plus, in Cumbria, 12-year-old motocross biker Arai is flown across the country for emergency surgery on a deep chest wound

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Ninety-one-year-old Cyril is airlifted to hospital after a car crash which required him to be cut from his car. Meanwhile, the medical team treat 57-year-old Caroline, who arrives at St Georges with chest pains, plus five-year-old Roman has a pencil stuck in his ear

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The Food Unwrapped team explore the snacks aisle. Kate Quilton visits Kenya to find out how many mangoes are in a snack pack, Helen Lawal learns the mystery of the fluffy centre of a snowball, John Whaite cracks open the secret of caramel popcorn, and Andi Oliver investigates what a supermarket aisle means when youre shopping online

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo head to Herefordshire to meet George, who has been collecting for more than 70 years. Max finds a crate of vintage motor oil bottles that he thinks could be worth a small fortune

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In the picturesque Devon countryside, Henry Cole and Simon OBrien meet Derek and Daphne, who have decades of collecting stored in multiple barns. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owners Tim and Amelia Whitaker welcome their guests to their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast, and take them out on a boat for a spot of seal watching and diving. However, the sight of wet suits proves too much for one contestant, and tensions also flare over dinner when Mike and Danny are quizzed about their relationship

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eva Buschmann hosts the competition at the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset. She makes the mistake of assuming Danny Robinson and Mike Gibson are a couple, and offers them a double room with an exposed shower, but hopes a lesson in abstract art and a meal at her favourite harbour-side restaurant will win the other contestants over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike Gibson and Danny Robinson host their rival BandB owners at their four-star guesthouse - Homecliffe Hotel in Blackpool. The hosts take their visitors on a tram ride along the seafront and on to Madame Tussauds, but there are complaints about uncomfortable mattresses and disappointing views and the next days breakfast fails to impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and air their grievances. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie start the proceedings, but confront the guests who complained about their room having no en-suite facilities, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five cooks from in and around Harrogate, North Yorkshire, battle to win the £1,000 prize in the culinary contest. First to take on hosting duties is farm girl Jane Steele, who hopes a combination of innovative recipes and fancy wine will win over her guests - horse enthusiast Cassy Bell, photographer Simon Meyer, country gent Alasdair Moore and adventurous punk and cupcake seller Cathy McConaghy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast Cassy Bell hosts the second dinner party in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, hoping her jockey-themed evening and gastro-pub menu will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cathy McConaghy hosts the third dinner party in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, where she decides to return to her Irish roots and serve a menu of home-made soda bread, cockle soup, oysters, roast lamb, and stout-infused sticky toffee pudding as she vies for the £1,000 prize. However, not all of her guests are impressed with the choice of food being dished up

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Simon Meyer is the fourth dinner-party host in Harrogate, North Yorkshire. He takes a laid-back approach to the task, but his chicken soup starter goes down so well the guests comments leave him struggling to retain his composure

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Country gent Alasdair Moore hosts the final dinner party in Harrogate, and hopes to impress his guests with his specially selected wines and proper menu. While his monkfish is well received, an early slip-up dents his confidence, and a risky parlour game does not endear him to farm lady Jane Steele. As the evening progresses, his competitors rate their experience, before the winner of the £1,000 cash prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Black Lion in Halland, East Sussex, where host Nigel Fright gets off to a shaky start when a kettle goes missing from one of the rooms. Later, he takes the guests to a salsa class, where Sherrill Tacy and Hayley Cotton are surprised by some of his dance moves

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to the Wheatsheaf Inn in Exning, near Newmarket, Suffolk, where guests are welcomed by owner Ken Clutterbuck and employee Ginny Trow. The proprietor tries to win his rivals round with his unique sense of humour, but tensions rise when the contestants go for a spin on a go-kart track

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sherrill Tacy hosts at the Sherwood Guesthouse in New Brighton on the Wirral, and is praised for the establishments value for money. However, there is upset when her breakfast leaves a lot to be desired, and her final review is far from perfect

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Final hosts Mark and Rachael Dann welcome the contestants to the Big Bear Lodge in Shropshire. They face the tough job of trying to keep the rift between some of the guests to a minimum in their ski-themed BandB, and their afternoon activity of canoeing raises a few laughs but accusations of cheating occur when the boys begin to get competitive

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help first-time buyers from Manchester. Robyn and Alice are struggling to decide between a starter flat and classic Corrie-style redbrick home. Steph and Christopher have been outbid 10 times even though theyve bid as much as £50,000 over asking price

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go head to head to help a couple who cannot decide to do with their home in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire. Procurement manager Paul bought the house 13 years ago as a bachelor pad, but since cabin crew worker Sophie moved in they have had two children and he wants to move to a bigger, better family home. However, she grew up on the same street, her parents are just down the road and she loves having them on hand for babysitting. Can Kirstie make the necessary renovations to persuade Paul to stay? Or will Phil find a new good enough home to tempt Sophie away?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Marcus and Abi, who are building a luxurious off-grid family home deep in 20-acre woodland in Pembrokeshire on a budget of just £100,000. Granted permission through the One Planet Development planning laws, which are unique to Wales, they are armed with determination and timber from the surrounding forest. With no detailed plans, the project pushes the limits of design and endurance, and proves a raw, physical adventure that defies convention and challenges many of the ideas around what a home should be

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

programme.desc: The owners of the musically themed Harmony BandB in Torquay, Devon, begin the competition, trying to prove their establishment offers the best value for money. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the weeks competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Francis Leigh hosts the next dinner party in Winchester, hoping to dazzle his guests with his sunburnt chicken curry, a Thai meal with a twist and a dessert simply called sticky. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Chernobyl: The New Evidence
programme.desc: Using previously classified KGB documents, the second part of this film explores how far Soviet leaders were willing to go to cover up the Chernobyl nuclear disaster of 1986, including exposing children to toxic fallout at the Kyiv May Day parade to prove to the world that all was fine. Featuring first-hand accounts from those who survived the blast, including Oleksiy Ananenko, who braved radioactive waters to prevent a deadlier second explosion and Maryna Sivets, whose unborn childs life was put at risk

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton tours Outer Orkney, beginning at North Ronaldsay, which may be the most northerly of the Orkneys but turns out to be surprisingly easy to get to. Paul then hops across to Papa Stronsay, which is now populated by an order of monks who have chosen to make this isolated island their home, and ends on the island of Stroma in the Pentland Firth, a previously inhabited island that was abandoned in the 1950s. One of the last people to live there joins Paul to recall when it was still a thriving community

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton visits the islands of Coll and Tiree, which though just a few miles apart could not be more different. On Coll, he meets Nicolas Maclean Bristol, owner of Breachacha Castle, who can trace his ancestors on the island back to the 14th century. Original diaries and documents, along with rarely seen film and photographic material, illustrate the tragedy

programme.desc: Irita Marriott and Ishy Khan search for antiques and artefacts in Hingham, Norfolk, the ancestral home of the 16th president of the USA, Abraham Lincoln. programme.desc: Actress Lesley Manville researches her family tree. So far as her mothers side is concerned, by delving deep into Brightons local archives, Lesley uncovers a complex story of wartime separation, adultery and the inaccessibility of divorce. Tracing back her fathers line sees Lesley trying to solve what happened to her ancestor Aaron Harding. A widower with a large family to feed, Aaron protested poor conditions and pay as an agricultural worker in 1830 - a protest that would become known as the Swing Riots. He was arrested and subsequently transported to Australia

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Mark Hill and Izzie Balmer - Day 5
programme.desc: The last leg of their journey sees Izzie Balmer and Mark Hill set out from Buckinghamshire seeking treasures to take to auction. Mark gets his paws on a mid-century teddy bear, while Izzie is hypnotised by early 20th-century glass commemorating Glasgows International Exhibition of 1901. Needless to say, only one of the pair can reign victorious after their final auction

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: David Harper and Hettie Jago - Day 1
programme.desc: Debut road tripper Hettie Jago takes on regular dealer David Harper. The pair start out in Scotland with £1500 each and a 1980s Mercedes 280 SL

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell return to help more people reunite with long-lost relatives, beginning with a man whose last contact with his father was a brief phone call from America when he was six, and a woman who was transported from Bogota to Berkshire when her Colombian birth mother gave her up for adoption

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate the case of Maria Roberts, the daughter of Italian parents who had a happy adoption growing up with an English family, and now hopes to find Gilda, her birth mother. The team also meets Francesca Barnes, who is in the early stages of transitioning from male to female. She was born with the name Paul and given up for adoption as a baby. Francesca is now searching for her mother, Norma, but fears her transition may prove too much of a challenge, and lead to rejection

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and the team are in Derbyshire to help a widow clear out her home, where they uncover a riding crop, a 19th-century stool and a jewellery collection

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and the team clear out a huge aviation collection in Co Durham, then help a retired policeman sell items from his four-bedroom home

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Lesley Manville researches her family tree. So far as her mothers side is concerned, by delving deep into Brightons local archives, Lesley uncovers a complex story of wartime separation, adultery and the inaccessibility of divorce. Tracing back her fathers line sees Lesley trying to solve what happened to her ancestor Aaron Harding. A widower with a large family to feed, Aaron protested poor conditions and pay as an agricultural worker in 1830 - a protest that would become known as the Swing Riots. He was arrested and subsequently transported to Australia

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Nick Hewer, host of Countdown and Alan Sugars former adviser on The Apprentice, tries to find out how his mothers Catholic father Oswald Jamieson managed to reach the prestigious office of High Sheriff in Protestant-dominated 1920s Belfast. On his fathers side, he researches the life of his nine times great-grandfather and discovers how his familys fortunes were shaped by the English Civil War. Narrated by Cherie Lunghi

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Growing up, Barbara Jacobs always believed that she was one of three children. It was not until she reached her twenties that she made a discovery which would change her life. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate Barbaras story, and also Sharon Heartes. She was mixed race child adopted by a white family who grew up in the West Midlands. Sharon knew her birth mother, Barbara, was a white woman who worked in a Smethwick bicycle factory, and her father was from Jamaica. She wants to track down her mother to tell her that she understands why she was given up for adoption

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Angela Brennan believed she was adopted, but at 18 her mother revealed she had an older sister, also called Angela, who had been put up for adoption by her parents. Cameras follow Angelas search for her namesake sibling. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell also examine the story of Bronwen Hook. When her son, Mark, was born, she did her best to raise him, but Bronwen found being responsible for a baby hard, and gave him up for adoption. Shes always hoped she would see Mark again. Will she finally get her chance?

programme.desc: Hugh Wallace meets newlyweds Eleanor and David Shannon, who have bought the Old Presbytery in Caltra, County Galway, and plan to spend their first year as a married couple renovating this property. David takes the technology route to DIY upskilling by using AI and YouTube tutorials. Healthcare worker Eleanor is getting first-hand training in lime plastering

programme.title: Red Eye
programme.desc: Tensions rise between Hana, Brody and Ambassador Tillman over whether the guests death was murder or a drunken accident. When Fox kills again, it proves Hana was right and that hes targeting the Ambassadors guests. Tennant is brought in to lead operations as Megan links both victims to testimony about a Russian sub downing the Samson D-300. Delaney presses Tillman and Peterson, who also testified, on why they blamed Russia. Shes troubled to learn an engineer who reported faults died suspiciously. Seeking answers, Hana goes to Tillmans office and ends up trapped in a lift with Fox-a ride she may not survive

programme.title: Any Given Day: Cork University Hospital
programme.desc: A patient having a heart attack is on his way to CUH from University Hospital Waterford, and in the paediatric allergy clinic, 11-year-old Ciaran is in for an oral food challenge. The emergency department team is standing by as a five-year-old girl is rushed to CUH. She is barely conscious and her vitals are poor. Surgeon James Harty is prepares for major surgery - the total replacement of a patients femur

programme.title: On the Beat
programme.desc: The massive challenges being faced by Gardaí across Ireland, concluding with a sensitive case in Monaghan in which a young woman is being extorted for money. Plus, the officers dealing with the growing shoplifting problem across the country

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Architect Dermot Bannon and quantity surveyor Claire Irwin help empty nesters Deirdre and Kieran Kelly at Clonsilla in west Dublin. To give Deirdre and Kieran the space they need, Dermot sets out to combine inside and outside with a triple-zoned ground floor, a glass corner extension and clever landscaping

programme.title: Reality Bites
programme.desc: Colm Flynn embarks on a personal journey to Belarus, to discover what happened to the little boy his family once tried to adopt. Now, Colm follows Bannis trail back to Belarus, hoping to find out what became of the lad who was almost his little brother

Oscar Wilde comedy drama, with Cate Blanchett, Minnie Driver, Rupert Everett, Jeremy Northam and Julianne Moore

programme.title: Greys Anatomy
programme.desc: Richard tackles a shocking case on his first day back. Kavita and Jules prepare for a high-stakes presentation. Bailey has a difficult conversation with a beloved patient

programme.title: Bad Nanny
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary telling the story of Samantha Cookes - serial fraudster, master manipulator and, at one point, a trusted nanny. The film reveals one of her most brazen personas - Carrie Jade Williams, a supposedly terminally ill, award-winning author - which she used to scam her way across the UK and Ireland. She also invented the character of a nanny with a troubling secret, as well as several other audacious cons through the years

programme.title: Shortland Street
programme.desc: Maeve makes a promise she cant keep, Jack suffers the consequences, and Madonna takes a starring role

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Abigail and Mali are at odds, Sonny spends the day with John, and Tane argues his case on the bridge in front of Brax

programme.title: Asia: Making of
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at this dazzling exploration of Earths biggest and most varied continent, Asia

programme.title: Na Feilte Tine
programme.desc: Documentary exploring four indigenous Irish festivals of Samhain, Imbolg, Bealtaine and Lunasa. Each festival acts as a welcome to the new season. This edition takes a look at the festival of Samhain at the beginning of the Celtic new year, featuring a re-enactment of the ritual of the First Fire on the sacred hill of Tlachta

programme.title: Ambulance (2022)
programme.desc: Decorated veteran Will Sharp, desperate for money to cover his wifes medical bills, asks for help from the one person he knows he shouldnt - his adoptive brother Danny, a charismatic career criminal. Danny instead offers him a score - the biggest bank heist in Los Angeles history. With his wifes survival on the line, Will cant say no, but when their getaway goes spectacularly wrong, the desperate brothers hijack an ambulance with a wounded cop and an EMT onboard. Crime drama, directed by Michael Bay and starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Yahya Abdul-Mateen II and Eiza González

programme.title: Ireland in Music
programme.desc: Berlin-based singer Wallis Bird travels to Kells Courthouse in her native County Meath to share brand new songs and stories with presenter Denise Chaila

programme.title: Shakes Versus Shav (2023)
programme.desc: Short puppet movie based on the last play completed by George Bernard Shaw, in which the Irish playwright battles English bard William Shakespeare in a war of words to decide which of them is the better writer. Voiced by Colm Meaney and Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr fight against their maximalist taste to create a calming Balearic bedroom. The pair settle on an all-white, exposed natural brick feel. To nail their vision, the duo visit BandB rivals and local friends Ian and Andrews Ibiza-inspired holiday let, where bespoke storage and an innovative approach to the use of plaster get their creative juices flowing. Swapping out the boiler suits for Lycra, the pair later scope out Moclins famous cycling routes for future guests for the BandB and later still head to the Strait of Gibraltar for the trip of a lifetime

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr reach the last room of the Andalusian townhouse, deciding to go all-out designing a Moorish-themed bar. Amanda refuses to hold back and is ready and willing to bring in some opulence, keen to drench the room in patterned wallpaper and fabulous gold touches. Taking a break from the property, the pair head to a traditional tea house in the oldest neighbourhood in Granada - and get more than they bargained for when theyre treated to a surprise belly dance performance. Later, they reveal their completed labour of love with a big farewell party for their friends and family.

programme.title: Shortland Street
programme.desc: Maeve plays the hero, TK makes the wrong move, and Esther keeps a secret

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Remi gears up for his surgery, Sonny is locked in a deep conversation with Dana, and Cash arrives at work to learn Tane hasnt checked in yet

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The search for the best patisserie team in Britain concludes, as Liam Charles and host Stacey Solomon welcome teams of pastry chefs hailing from establishments in Manchester, Cumbria, Wiltshire, Birmingham and London. As always, they face two days of testing challenges judged by world-class pâtissier experts Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden. Its the grand finale, and the remaining three teams pour all their patisserie passion, prowess and precision into this final epic challenge

programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Master builder Harrison Gardner meets self-employed circus performers Aoife and Tony, who have bought a property in rural Co Westmeath that requires a total upgrade. However with only 50,000 euros to spend to renovate and extend their new home, they have no choice but to take on the project themselves - with Harrison by their side every step of the way

programme.title: Here We Go
programme.desc: Amy gets spotted by a modelling agency talent scout, which inspires Cherry and Robin to cash in on their new baby by getting him signed up for some child modelling too. Rachel finds herself the victim of an online scam and decides to track down and confront the scammers face to face. Luckily, Paul is a highly trained police officer, well-suited to confrontations with potentially dangerous criminals

programme.title: First Dates Ireland
programme.desc: Argentinian recruitment executive Luciana from Rathmines, who claims to have a personality like sweet and sour chicken, spices it up with security expert and former soldier Darragh from Balbriggan. Wexford physiotherapist Carl is looking for an emotional connection - and may have found it with outgoing Eoin from Stoneybatter who has bigger hands than anyone hes ever met. Nurse Fiona is paired with former nurse Tom, and Toronto-born Mariah meets musician Conor, whose fear of blind dates is matched only by an ability to charm women by playing guitar

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Erin, Clare, Michelle, Orla and James are beside themselves with excitement as they have tickets to see boyband Take That in Belfast. When Ma Mary forbids them to go, they decide that they will stop at nothing to get there and see their idols in concert. Comedy from writer Lisa McGee, set against the Troubles in early-1990s Northern Ireland, starring Saoirse-Monica Jackson, Nicola Coughlan, Jamie-Lee ODonnell, Louisa Harland and Dylan Llewellyn

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The unit is called in to investigate when a lieutenant returns home on the eve of Halloween to find a surprise party being thrown for someone else at his place. Sean Murray, David McCallum and Mark Harmon star

programme.title: Did You Read About Erskine Fogarty?
programme.desc: Robert Sheenan stars in this darkly absurd comedy based on Blindboy Boatclubs acclaimed short story. It follows a day in the life of a man who has lost everything in the financial crash, as he drags his last possession in the world - a large American-style fridge - through the streets of Limerick, while gradually losing touch with reality

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job

programme.title: The Tommy Tiernan Show
